title: Fluentd and Fluent Bit TLS Certificate Errors on Output Connections (HEC, Forwarders, Cloud Endpoints) (handshake failures, trust bundle drift, hostname mismatch, weak ciphers, mutual authentication misuse, pinning, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.1.15 Splunk server-side certificate inventory and splunkd trust narratives, UC-13.7.1 buffer saturation without Transport Layer Security evidence, UC-13.7.4 worker crash detection, UC-13.7.5 proactive buffer fill forecasting, or generic network reachability tests that ignore engine error semantics. Does not rotate certificates automatically.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake with fifteen minute schedule; platform logging dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab logging namespace, mount an incomplete CA bundle for a non-production Splunk ingestion URL while collectors continue scraping metrics, confirm fluentd:logs and fluent_bit:logs arms emit verify errors, retry metrics rise on the affected output_id, and observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within thirty minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring the correct trust bundle, rolling the DaemonSet, and validating openssl s_client success from inside the pod, confirm tls_error_count_15min falls below thresholds, severity returns to low or medium, and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

dataSources: (index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs OR sourcetype=fluentd:metrics) OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError, SSL_connect, SSL_read, verify error, unable to write data, Failed to flush, failed_certificate_verify, certificate has expired, Hostname does not match; (index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs) [error] [tls], tls handshake failed, failed to verify peer certificate, peer did not return a certificate; (index=k8s sourcetype=kube:logs source=*fluent* OR source=*splunk*) outbound DNS and TLS context; (index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner) bundle_age_days and leaf days_to_expiry snapshots; lookup fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv keyed on output_id; cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata for pipeline_region
app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud + Splunk Connect for Kubernetes (optional) + fluent-plugin-prometheus or monitoring agent + Calyptia Fluent Bit or Fluentd (optional)
spl:
`comment("UC-13.7.9 Fluentd Fluent Bit outbound TLS to Splunk ingestion and cloud sinks; earliest=-4h multisearch five lanes")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"openssl::ssl::sslerror|error_class=openssl::ssl::sslerror|ssl_connect|ssl_read|unable to write data|failed to flush|failed_certificate_verify|certificate has expired|hostname does not match the server certificate|verify error|handshake failure|certificate verify failed|wrong version number|tlsv1 alert protocol version")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,out_plugin,match_output,"unmapped_output")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest_url,hec_url,endpoint,forward_host,splunk_server,"unknown_destination"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname does not match|name mismatch|cert hostname|san mismatch|certificate for"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"deprecated|weak cipher|cipher_suite|sslv3|tlsv1 alert protocol|insecure|downgrad|ecdhe_rsa with"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,plugin_retry,"0")),10)
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ca_bundle_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(leaf_days_to_expiry,cert_days_left,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentd_log_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"\[error\].*\[tls\]|tls handshake failed|failed to verify the peer's certificate|peer did not return a certificate|certificate verify failed|x509|ssl error")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_name,section,"fluent_bit_out")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_dest,upstream,forward_host,"unknown_destination"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname|name mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"cipher|protocol version|alert"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries,retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ca_bundle_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(leaf_days_to_expiry,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="fluent_bit_log_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where match(metric,"retry|fluentd_output_status_retry|output_status_retry")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,"fluentd_retry_metric")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest,destination,hec_cluster,"metrics_destination"))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value,_value,fluentd_output_status_retry_count,retry_count,"1")),10)
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=0
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=0
      | eval days_to_expiry=999
      | eval lane="fluentd_metrics_retry_tls_proxy"
      | where retry_recent>0
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=k8s sourcetype=kube:logs (source=*fluent* OR source=*splunk*) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"tls|ssl|certificate|handshake|x509|verify|bad certificate|unknown ca|ingest.*http")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.pod_name,pod,host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,container_name,"k8s_fluent_shipper")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(downstream_host,dest_host,"k8s_downstream"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname|mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"cipher|protocol|alert"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=0
      | eval days_to_expiry=999
      | eval lane="kube_fluent_pod_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,trust_anchor_id,"ca_bundle_snapshot")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scanner_target,fqdn,endpoint_fqdn,"infra_tls_peer"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=0
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=0
      | eval retry_recent=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_age_days,ca_trust_age_days,trust_kit_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(days_to_expiry,dte,leaf_dte,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="cert_scanner_ca_pin"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,"unknown_destination")
| eval merge_key=strcat(host,"|",output_id,"|",destination)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=15 current=t global=f count AS tls_error_count_15min sum(tls_hostname_mm) AS hostname_mismatch_count sum(tls_weak_cipher) AS weak_cipher_count max(retry_recent) AS retry_count_recent last(ca_bundle_age_days) AS ca_bundle_age_days last(days_to_expiry) AS days_to_expiry BY merge_key
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| dedup merge_key
| eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,deployment_env,"production"))))
| eventstats median(tls_error_count_15min) AS env_tls_p50 BY environment
| eventstats dc(destination) AS distinct_destinations_failing BY host
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
     | rename destination AS inv_destination runbook_url AS runbook_lookup
     | fields output_id inv_destination owner_team ca_bundle_secret runbook_lookup criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup output_id
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rename owner_team AS meta_owner
      | fields output_id meta_owner pipeline_region ]
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,inv_destination,"unmapped_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(meta_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval severity=case(tls_error_count_15min>20 OR days_to_expiry<7 OR distinct_destinations_failing>=2,"critical", tls_error_count_15min>8 OR hostname_mismatch_count>=1 OR retry_count_recent>=100,"high", tls_error_count_15min>3 OR weak_cipher_count>=1 OR retry_count_recent>=25,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","emergency_validate_trust_anchor_roll_daemonset_reload_compare_openssl_x509_and_lb_sni", severity=="high","trace_intermediate_chain_proxy_mtls_and_pod_secret_mount_paths", severity=="medium","rotate_client_cert_or_widen_tls_versions_under_vendor_guidance", true(),"collect_tcpdump_sni_openssl_s_client_and_fluent_tls_stanzas")
| table _time host output_id destination owner_team criticality tls_error_count_15min hostname_mismatch_count days_to_expiry ca_bundle_age_days retry_count_recent weak_cipher_count severity recommended_action
description: Detects outbound Transport Layer Security handshake and trust failures from Fluentd, Fluent Bit, td-agent, Calyptia builds, and Splunk Connect for Kubernetes when dialing Splunk ingestion gateways, forwarders, or cloud sinks, by fusing engine logs, retry metrics, Kubernetes pod context, and certificate scanner snapshots, then ranking severity using sliding error density, hostname mismatch indicators, weak cipher language, mutual authentication gaps, and inventory-enriched ownership.
value: Platform and reliability leaders shorten mean time to innocence when logs thin out yet applications look healthy, because client-side trust breaks often precede buffer loss and appear as noisy handshake errors scattered across clusters. Risk and engineering governance groups receive a tamper-resistant narrative that separates forwarder trust drift from Splunk server certificate issues and routes remediation to the team that owns each output_id contract.
implementation: Land fluentd:logs, fluent_bit:logs, fluentd:metrics retry series, kube:logs from logging DaemonSets, and infra_certs cert_scanner snapshots; normalize output_id; maintain fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv; schedule observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake every fifteen minutes with earliest=-4h@h; join cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.
visualization: Severity-colored table by host and output_id with drilldowns to fluentd:logs, fluent_bit:logs, fluentd:metrics, kube:logs, and cert_scanner JSON; single-value tiles for distinct_destinations_failing; timeline of tls_error_count_15min with annotations for change windows.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Inventory.hostname) AS inv_host latest(Inventory.os) AS inv_os FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory.Inv_Device earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.ip
| rename Inventory.ip AS dest_ip
| join type=left max=0 dest_ip [| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Network_Resolution WHERE nodename=Network_Resolution earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Network_Resolution.dest | rename Network_Resolution.dest AS dest_ip]
| eval dest_ip=lower(trim(toString(dest_ip)))
| table dest_ip inv_host inv_os count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Inventory summaries for hosts and pods modeled as managed devices when correlating output_id to assets; accelerate Network_Resolution summaries when DNS forward and reverse mappings contextualize gateway names; retain thirty days for quarterly platform attestations.

references.title: Fluentd Documentation — Install Treasure Agent (td-agent) and OpenSSL notes
Splunk Docs — Use the HTTP Event Collector (TLS and tokens)
Splunk Connect for Kubernetes — documentation site
IETF RFC 8446 — The Transport Layer Security (TLS) Protocol Version 1.3

knownFalsePositives: Planned certificate rotation windows with overlapping validity can elevate verify noise until pods reload; require change_ticket correlation before paging application owners. Intentional development clusters that temporarily disable peer verification will surface insecure patterns; downgrade routing using environment labels rather than muting production. Downstream load balancer blue-green drains can present different certificates per slice; treat bursts as benign when traffic steering announcements match the timeline. Geographic content delivery splits may show divergent chains at different edges; pair tls_error_count_15min with regional health dashboards before blaming collectors. Vendor announced certificate migrations communicated through change management should not surprise on-call when runbook URLs already document expected retry ramps. Security scanners that probe logging namespaces can emit synthetic handshake failures in indices shared with production collectors; isolate scanner traffic or tag scanner_origin. Short-term kube log sampling gaps can empty the kube arm while other arms remain healthy; monitor collection heartbeats separately from alert severity. Stale fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv rows with wrong owner_team create noisy tickets; refresh lookups weekly. Duplicate events from redundant forwarders can inflate streamstats counts until dedupe summaries land; validate indexer receipt rules. Corporate proxy failovers may present a different issuing authority for minutes; confirm proxy high availability timelines before emergency PKI work.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on output_id entities by pipeline_region and owner_team

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team, public key infrastructure operators, Kubernetes platform engineers, and the teams that operate Splunk HTTP Event Collector ingress paths, forwarder receivers, and cloud observability endpoints. This use case isolates outbound Transport Layer Security failures that originate inside Fluentd open source collectors, Fluent Bit agents, Treasure Data style td-agent packages, Calyptia hardened Fluent builds, and Splunk Connect for Kubernetes chart components when they dial remote HTTPS or mutual authentication endpoints. It is deliberately not a Splunk server certificate inventory card: UC-13.1.15 remains authoritative for splunkd listener material, deployment server bundles, and indexer cluster trust stores. Here the client is the log shipper and the server is your ingestion gateway, managed load balancer, cloud vendor edge, Kafka broker, Elasticsearch coordinating node, Loki distributor, or object storage API that terminates TLS in front of the real workload.

Understanding the Ruby Fluentd transport model begins with the transport tls subsection that accompanies forward and http style outputs. Operators declare ssl_version or modern ssl_min_version and ssl_max_version pairs, ca_path or ca_file trust bundles, client cert_path and private_key_path pairs for mutual authentication, and insecure flags that must remain absent in production estates. Mis-copied sample configurations frequently leave verify_hostname false or disable peer verification during a rushed lab test, then propagate into production via Helm value merges. Fluent Bit uses parallel concepts with tls on, tls.verify on, tls.ca_file, tls.crt_file, tls.key_file, and tls.vhost for Server Name Indication when a single ingress serves many certificates. Splunk Connect for Kubernetes maps Helm values into Kubernetes secrets that mount certificate bundles under well known paths; a silent mismatch between secret key names and the path referenced by the generated Fluent Bit or Fluentd configuration produces handshake failures that look like network outages until you read the engine stderr.

Treasure Data td-agent builds bundle specific OpenSSL and Ruby combinations. Older long-term support hosts may negotiate only legacy protocols unless you raise ssl_min_version and rebuild container images. Federal Information Processing Standards mode on Linux can restrict cipher suites that still appear in vendor documentation examples, yielding alerts that read like protocol version mismatch even when certificates are fresh. Java virtual machine based sidecars are out of scope for this card yet they sometimes share the same pod network namespace; when troubleshooting, remember that JVM trust stores and operating system trust stores are distinct buckets and mixing them causes intermittent success depending on which process initiated the connection.

Certificate pinning and hard-coded digest checks appear in advanced automation that pins Splunk gateway certificates to prevent rogue ingress substitution. When operations renew a load balancer certificate with the same key but a new issuer chain, pinning logic may reject the new public key fingerprint. Calyptia enterprise distributions sometimes ship opinionated defaults that differ from upstream Fluent Bit; validate minor version release notes before assuming directive parity across distributions.

Scope boundaries keep sibling observability gold cards complementary. UC-13.7.1 focuses on buffer saturation and retry exhaustion that may follow any output stall including Transport Layer Security faults, yet buffer storytelling alone does not prove handshake failure. UC-13.7.4 focuses on worker crashes and supervisor respawn loops where process death can interrupt TLS sessions in misleading ways. UC-13.7.5 focuses on proactive buffer fill slopes before hard overflow. When UC-13.7.9 fires alongside UC-13.7.1, treat the pair as cause and effect until timelines prove independent receivers. When UC-13.7.9 fires with UC-13.7.4, separate native crashes from handshake errors by reading signal_class and stack traces before blaming remote gateways.

Risk briefing: planned maintenance on downstream gateways routinely elevates retry counters while engineers expect bounded loss; require dwell thresholds and change_ticket_id correlation before customer-facing pages. Penetration testing appliances that intercept corporate egress can present a different trust anchor than public roots; pods that trust only public bundles will fail until you inject the enterprise root into the projected secret volume. Legal reviewers should confirm whether raw engine excerpts may contain customer payloads; redact aggressively in pager templates while retaining full raw access for a break-glass role.

Differentiation recap: outbound client handshake failures, trust anchor drift, mutual authentication misuse, Server Name Indication mismatch, weak cipher downgrade language, certificate pinning rejections, and Calyptia or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes specific mount paths, not Splunk server.pem introspection from UC-13.1.15, not pure buffer math without Transport Layer Security evidence, not supervisor crash-first narratives from UC-13.7.4.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single exporter always works during incidents.

Arm Fluentd engine logs: ship Ruby Fluentd or td-agent stdout and file logs into index=fluentd with sourcetype=fluentd:logs. Preserve multiline OpenSSL stacks with generous LINE_BREAKER and TRUNCATE values. Capture SSLError class lines, unable to write data after transport failures, Failed to flush chunks, verify error tokens, certificate has expired phrases, and hostname mismatch language emitted by Ruby HTTP clients. Include structured fields when your forwarder adds output_id, plugin_id, dest_url, and retry_count at collection time.

Arm Fluent Bit logs: land container stderr into index=fluent_bit with sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs. Focus on bracketed error lines that mention tls, handshake failures, failed to verify peer certificates, and peer did not return certificate when mutual authentication is mandatory. Namespace and pod labels should flow into kubernetes.pod_name and cluster fields for stable joins.

Arm Fluentd Prometheus metrics: scrape fluentd_output_status_retry_count and related output health gauges into sourcetype=fluentd:metrics on index=fluentd. Align scrape intervals with log collection so retry spikes correlate with error lines without expensive joins across hour boundaries.

Arm Kubernetes pod logs: forward kubelet collected logs from logging namespaces into index=k8s with sourcetype=kube:logs and source paths that include fluent or splunk connect strings. This arm surfaces DNS lookup failures, dial timeouts, and proxy environment variables that precede pure certificate errors.

Arm infrastructure certificate snapshots: ingest modular input or scanner output into index=infra_certs with sourcetype=cert_scanner that records bundle_age_days, leaf days_to_expiry, and trust_anchor identifiers keyed to output_id where possible. This arm distinguishes stale projected ConfigMaps from genuinely expired downstream gateway certificates.

Maintain fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv with columns output_id, destination, owner_team, ca_bundle_secret, runbook_url, and criticality. Refresh weekly or automate replication from configuration management databases. props.conf should strip ANSI color codes, normalize UTC timestamps, and map vendor specific field aliases into the coalesce ladders used by the saved search.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
openssl s_client -connect hec.example.com:443 -servername hec.example.com -showcerts </dev/null | openssl x509 -noout -dates -subject -issuer
kubectl -n logging describe secret splunk-hec-tls | sed -n '1,120p'
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/fluent-bit --tail=200 | rg -i "tls|ssl|x509"
/opt/td-agent/bin/td-agent --dry-run -c /etc/td-agent/td-agent.conf
```

Pre-save validation: each arm must return events in lab within one hour of enabling collection. Induce a harmless trust break against a non-production gateway and confirm all five arms move within one collection interval.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after tuning for fleet noise. Throttle duplicate host, output_id pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd engine logs, Fluent Bit logs, Fluentd retry metrics, Kubernetes pod context, and infrastructure certificate snapshots so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine trust crisis. coalesce ladders absorb host, output_id, and destination naming drift across virtual machines and pods. streamstats measures a sliding event window per merge_key to approximate fifteen minute TLS error density together with hostname mismatch and weak cipher indicators. eventstats adds fleet median context and distinct destination failure counts per host for blast-radius estimation. case maps combined signals into critical, high, medium, and low tiers aligned with platform policy. inputlookup enriches output_id with owner_team, criticality, and runbook URLs while join adds configuration management metadata from fluentd_pipeline_metadata for pipeline_region coverage.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.9 Fluentd Fluent Bit outbound TLS to Splunk ingestion and cloud sinks; earliest=-4h multisearch five lanes")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"openssl::ssl::sslerror|error_class=openssl::ssl::sslerror|ssl_connect|ssl_read|unable to write data|failed to flush|failed_certificate_verify|certificate has expired|hostname does not match the server certificate|verify error|handshake failure|certificate verify failed|wrong version number|tlsv1 alert protocol version")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,out_plugin,match_output,"unmapped_output")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest_url,hec_url,endpoint,forward_host,splunk_server,"unknown_destination"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname does not match|name mismatch|cert hostname|san mismatch|certificate for"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"deprecated|weak cipher|cipher_suite|sslv3|tlsv1 alert protocol|insecure|downgrad|ecdhe_rsa with"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,plugin_retry,"0")),10)
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ca_bundle_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(leaf_days_to_expiry,cert_days_left,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="fluentd_log_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"\[error\].*\[tls\]|tls handshake failed|failed to verify the peer's certificate|peer did not return a certificate|certificate verify failed|x509|ssl error")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_name,section,"fluent_bit_out")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_dest,upstream,forward_host,"unknown_destination"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname|name mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"cipher|protocol version|alert"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries,retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(ca_bundle_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(leaf_days_to_expiry,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="fluent_bit_log_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric,metric_name,name,""))))
      | where match(metric,"retry|fluentd_output_status_retry|output_status_retry")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,plugin_id,"fluentd_retry_metric")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dest,destination,hec_cluster,"metrics_destination"))))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval retry_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(value,_value,fluentd_output_status_retry_count,retry_count,"1")),10)
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=0
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=0
      | eval days_to_expiry=999
      | eval lane="fluentd_metrics_retry_tls_proxy"
      | where retry_recent>0
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=k8s sourcetype=kube:logs (source=*fluent* OR source=*splunk*) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"tls|ssl|certificate|handshake|x509|verify|bad certificate|unknown ca|ingest.*http")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kubernetes.pod_name,pod,host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,container_name,"k8s_fluent_shipper")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(downstream_host,dest_host,"k8s_downstream"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=if(match(lr,"hostname|mismatch|san"),1,0)
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=if(match(lr,"cipher|protocol|alert"),1,0)
      | eval retry_recent=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=0
      | eval days_to_expiry=999
      | eval lane="kube_fluent_pod_tls"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
    [ search index=infra_certs sourcetype=cert_scanner earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,src_host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,trust_anchor_id,"ca_bundle_snapshot")))
      | eval destination=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(scanner_target,fqdn,endpoint_fqdn,"infra_tls_peer"))))
      | eval tls_hostname_mm=0
      | eval tls_weak_cipher=0
      | eval retry_recent=0
      | eval ca_bundle_age_days=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(bundle_age_days,ca_trust_age_days,trust_kit_age_days,"0")),10)
      | eval days_to_expiry=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(days_to_expiry,dte,leaf_dte,"999")),10)
      | eval lane="cert_scanner_ca_pin"
      | fields _time host output_id destination tls_hostname_mm tls_weak_cipher retry_recent ca_bundle_age_days days_to_expiry lane ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,"unknown_destination")
| eval merge_key=strcat(host,"|",output_id,"|",destination)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=15 current=t global=f count AS tls_error_count_15min sum(tls_hostname_mm) AS hostname_mismatch_count sum(tls_weak_cipher) AS weak_cipher_count max(retry_recent) AS retry_count_recent last(ca_bundle_age_days) AS ca_bundle_age_days last(days_to_expiry) AS days_to_expiry BY merge_key
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| dedup merge_key
| eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,deployment_env,"production"))))
| eventstats median(tls_error_count_15min) AS env_tls_p50 BY environment
| eventstats dc(destination) AS distinct_destinations_failing BY host
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
     | rename destination AS inv_destination runbook_url AS runbook_lookup
     | fields output_id inv_destination owner_team ca_bundle_secret runbook_lookup criticality ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup output_id
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rename owner_team AS meta_owner
      | fields output_id meta_owner pipeline_region ]
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,inv_destination,"unmapped_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(meta_owner,owner_team,"unowned")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval severity=case(tls_error_count_15min>20 OR days_to_expiry<7 OR distinct_destinations_failing>=2,"critical", tls_error_count_15min>8 OR hostname_mismatch_count>=1 OR retry_count_recent>=100,"high", tls_error_count_15min>3 OR weak_cipher_count>=1 OR retry_count_recent>=25,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","emergency_validate_trust_anchor_roll_daemonset_reload_compare_openssl_x509_and_lb_sni", severity=="high","trace_intermediate_chain_proxy_mtls_and_pod_secret_mount_paths", severity=="medium","rotate_client_cert_or_widen_tls_versions_under_vendor_guidance", true(),"collect_tcpdump_sni_openssl_s_client_and_fluent_tls_stanzas")
| table _time host output_id destination owner_team criticality tls_error_count_15min hostname_mismatch_count days_to_expiry ca_bundle_age_days retry_count_recent weak_cipher_count severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_9_fluent_outbound_tls_handshake | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on merge_key, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap wildcard kube log volume with namespace filters after security agrees you are not dropping rare evidence rows.

RBAC and secrets hygiene for this control deserves explicit treatment because Transport Layer Security material is both sensitive and operationally fragile. Collectors that read Kubernetes secrets for projected bundles must use dedicated service accounts confined to logging namespaces. Rotate Data Encryption Key backed secrets with overlap windows that exceed the longest Fluent Bit chunk retention you allow, so pods never read half-written pem files during atomic swaps. NetworkPolicy should permit egress only to approved gateway subnets and broker lists declared in fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv, which doubles as an egress allowlist reference for firewall reviews. When Splunk Cloud or managed gateways front customer traffic, keep separate inventory rows for public names versus internal origin names so analysts do not conflate success on one layer with failure on another.

Parser and field normalization guidance: unify kubernetes.pod_name, pod, and host keys into the host column at ingest time when feasible to reduce coalesce fan-out in interactive searches. Map plugin_id from Fluentd monitor_agent polls into output_id when engineers standardize naming; otherwise accept unmapped_output labels and fix inventory rows rather than muting alerts. Preserve lane fields in summary indexes when you materialize arms hourly so Job Inspector traces remain intelligible during regressions. Avoid stripping OpenSSL reason codes from _raw during security hardening projects; those tokens often disambiguate hostname mismatch from unknown authority faster than secondary joins.

Dashboard design: host a matrix by cluster and owner_team with severity-colored rows, sparklines of tls_error_count_15min, and drilldowns to raw engine logs, kube pod excerpts, and cert_scanner JSON. Single-value tiles can track distinct_destinations_failing per fleet to show blast radius. Optional Splunk ITSI episodes may bind KPIs to counts of critical rows per pipeline_region when your organization already models logging pipelines as services.

Governance overlays: pair alert outputs with change_ticket_id fields when maintenance touches trust bundles, gateway profiles, or Kafka listener policies. Archive weekly CSV exports of fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv to a restricted evidence index for auditors who require proof of ownership mapping. Document explicit waiver language for laboratory clusters that deliberately break trust during negative testing so waiver metadata suppresses customer-facing routes without hiding engineering telemetry.

Advanced mutual authentication scenarios: when Fluentd presents a client certificate to a Splunk forwarder receiver or to a mutual authentication gateway, validate extended key usage and subject distinguished name patterns expected by the server. Revocation checks via Online Certificate Status Protocol or certificate revocation list endpoints may be blocked by firewall policy; failures surface as ambiguous handshake errors unless you capture secondary tcpdump evidence. For Calyptia Fleet managed agents, confirm centralized bundles propagate to edge nodes before on-call declares success; latency between control plane publication and node pull can exceed one collection interval.

Cloud vendor nuances: managed Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoints may rotate chains with short-lived intermediates more aggressively than on-premises estates. Object storage gateways and vendor-specific logging sinks sometimes require regional endpoint hostnames distinct from global aliases; Server Name Indication must match the hostname embedded in the certificate presented at that region. Content delivery networks in front of ingestion URLs can cause split-brain certificate presentations during partial cutovers; correlate tls_error_count_15min spikes with content delivery maintenance bulletins before blaming collectors.

Kafka and Elasticsearch footnotes: brokers that require client certificates use Java trust stores distinct from Fluentd OpenSSL paths; when dual-writing, ensure both outputs carry coherent trust bundles rather than copying one stanza verbatim without path adjustments. Elasticsearch clusters behind Elastic cloud may present Let's Encrypt chains that require updated root programs on older collectors; treat weak_cipher_count and protocol mismatch language as upgrade signals.

Closing operational guidance: rehearse this saved search quarterly with tabletop exercises that include PKI, Kubernetes platform, and observability leads. Rotate runbook URLs in fluentd_tls_endpoint_inventory.csv when teams reorganize so pager deep links do not rot. Keep openssl version matrices for td-agent and Fluent Bit container bases in the same knowledge base article referenced by recommended_action tokens to shorten bridge time during incidents.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the failing pod or virtual machine. Compare openssl s_client handshakes against the same hostname and port your output plugin uses, including Server Name Indication, and verify the returned chain matches the trust bundle mounted inside the container. When openssl succeeds from a bastion but fails inside the pod, suspect missing enterprise roots, stale projected secrets, or HTTP proxy environment variables that send traffic through an intercepting appliance.

Cross-check Splunk gateway health independently: UC-13.1.15 may show clean listener certificates while this card still fires because the shipper pins an old digest or trusts an outdated bundle. Correlate retry metrics with gateway maintenance windows and cloud provider incident timelines before declaring customer impact.

Synthetic validation: in a lab namespace, mount an intentionally incomplete chain into a Fluent Bit sidecar, confirm multisearch arms surface verify errors within two evaluation windows, then restore the correct bundle and confirm severity returns to low.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after rotations complete, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Clock skew validation: compare pod timestamps to authoritative network time protocol sources; notBefore errors often trace to drifted edge nodes rather than broken certificates.

Runbook linkage: document expected bursts during mutual authentication rotations and Kafka broker rolling upgrades so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved train.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Splunk ingestion gateway certificate expired over a weekend while forwarders still trusted the old bundle: emergency renew the public chain, update projected Kubernetes secrets atomically, roll logging DaemonSets in vendor-supported order, and validate handshake success with openssl before closing bridges.

Case 2 — Operators replaced the corporate trust bundle ConfigMap but skipped DaemonSet rollout: confirm mounted volume generations inside pods, force a controlled restart, and verify file mtimes align with the change ticket timestamp.

Case 3 — Intermediate issuer missing from the bundle mounted in pods while browsers succeed using cached intermediates: rebuild the pem file with full chain ordering, reload collectors without disabling verification, and regression test Loki and Elasticsearch outputs too.

Case 4 — Outbound web proxy performs Transport Layer Security interception with an internal certificate authority not present in container trust stores: inject the corporate root using a dedicated projected secret, document exception approvals, and validate proxy connect tunnels separately from direct handshakes.

Case 5 — Mutual authentication client certificate revoked or replaced without updating the private key pair paths: rotate Fluentd cert_path and private_key_path together, ensure Calyptia upstream definitions reference the same filenames, and confirm forwarder receivers expect the new issuer.

Case 6 — Federal Information Processing Standards constrained nodes reject modern cipher suites negotiated by a renewed cloud gateway: align ssl_min_version and cipher policies across Fluentd, Fluent Bit, and OpenSSL builds, or request a compatible gateway profile from the cloud vendor.

Case 7 — Server Name Indication missing on Fluent Bit tls.vhost while the load balancer serves multi-tenant certificates: add explicit vhost values matching DNS, retest with openssl s_client -servername, and verify the correct leaf is returned per ingress rule.

Case 8 — Edge node clock skewed minutes ahead of reality causing notBefore errors: fix network time protocol on the node, restart collectors after skew correction, and add adjacent time hygiene alerts to prevent repeat incidents.

Case 9 — Cloud edge rotates to elliptic curve certificates that older OpenSSL builds on legacy td-agent hosts cannot validate: upgrade collector images or add a compatibility terminating proxy with algorithms both sides accept under change control.

Case 10 — Kafka brokers mandate Transport Layer Security 1.3 while an older td-agent build negotiates only 1.2: upgrade Fluentd gems and OpenSSL dependencies, or temporarily relax broker listener policies only in lab with explicit governance approval.

Case 11 — Kubernetes secret misnamed keys cause the wrong file to mount as ca_file: compare Helm template key names to live mounts with kubectl describe, fix naming drift, and add pre-deploy schema tests in continuous integration.

Case 12 — Downstream load balancer renewed while pinning automation inside a sidecar still references the prior public key hash: update pin databases, redeploy sidecars, and document pin rotation runbooks alongside gateway renewals.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists fourteen analyst-facing columns including owner_team, criticality, and recommended_action tokens aligned to inventory enrichment.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the messengers at the loading dock: their gate pass is the server's identity card. When the card expired, the photo no longer matches the name on the badge, or the scanner rejects the chip, the truck cannot leave with your packages. We catch that rejection noise early so crews fix trust, clocks, and certificates before logs silently pile up out of sight.
hardware: Linux virtual machines running Fluentd or td-agent under systemd, Kubernetes worker nodes running Fluent Bit or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes DaemonSets, and hybrid estates forwarding to Splunk ingestion, forwarders, Kafka, Elasticsearch, Loki, or cloud object endpoints over TLS.
